page: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz, delivering 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The system is equipped with 384 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. The server excels in memory bandwidth, achieving top-tier (top 10%) performance in cached small-block read operations and strong (top 25%) performance in large-block read and write operations. Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions and encryption, rank in the top 10%, reflecting the impact of the hardware-accelerated instruction sets. Static web serving throughput also reaches top-tier levels, while single-core performance generally falls into the average to strong categories.

This instance is highly capable for workloads requiring significant parallel processing and memory throughput without the need for local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU acceleration. It demonstrates strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and medium-model LLM inference prompt processing. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for I/O-heavy applications. The resource density and high-speed DDR5 memory provide a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for enterprise-scale general-purpose workloads, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture and Nitro hypervisor, it includes 384 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 4 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show top-tier results in static web serving, memory bandwidth for cached small blocks, and Passmark CPU encryption tests. Multi-core processing and database operations rank in the top 25%, making this server highly efficient for parallel computing. It is well-suited for enterprise web serving, software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks where local storage is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose server featuring ninety-six vCPUs, fast DDR5 memory, and advanced hardware acceleration for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C CPU.
384 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (4 GB per core).
Top-tier performance in static web serving and cached memory bandwidth.
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage.
Suited for enterprise web serving, databases, and multi-threaded applications.
